title: South Indian Vegetable Curry

overview.description: A fragrant South Indian mixed vegetable curry with coconut, curry leaves, mustard seeds and green chilli.

ingredients: 2 tbsp coconut oil or neutral oil
400g mixed vegetables, cut into bite-sized pieces
method: Heat the oil in a large saucepan over a medium heat.
Add the mustard seeds and cook until they begin to pop.
Add the curry leaves carefully, as they may splutter.
Add the sliced onion and cook until softened.
Add the green chillies, garlic and ginger.
Add the turmeric, ground coriander and ground cumin.
Add the mixed vegetables and stir to coat in the spices.
Add the coconut milk, water and salt.
Cover and cook until the vegetables are tender but not falling apart.
Remove the lid and simmer briefly if the sauce needs thickening.
Scatter with fresh coriander before serving.

learning.did-you-know: South Indian cooking uses ingredients such as curry leaves, coconut, mustard seeds and tamarind in ways that give its dishes a distinctive character compared with many North Indian curries.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to temper spices in hot oil, build flavour with curry leaves and mustard seeds, and cook vegetables in a light coconut-based sauce.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A flexible South Indian vegetable dish that introduces the children to the distinctive flavours of mustard seeds, curry leaves and coconut.
chapman-tips: Add the mustard seeds to hot oil and wait for them to pop before adding the other ingredients.
Curry leaves can splutter dramatically when added to hot oil, so add them carefully.
Use vegetables with similar cooking times, or add harder vegetables before softer ones.
Don't boil the coconut milk aggressively or the sauce can become less smooth.
A squeeze of lime at the end lifts the richness of the coconut.
Try different vegetable combinations depending on what is available.
notes.batch-cooking: Cool completely before freezing in portions. The vegetables may soften slightly when reheated, but the curry freezes well.
notes.leftovers: Reheat gently and serve with rice, chapati or dosa. Leftover curry can also be used as a filling for wraps or stuffed flatbreads.
